General.a: SkillNest is a project-based online educational platform offering computer basics, coding, and AI classes for school students.
SkillNest was founded in 2024 by Aanandita Uplopwar, an experienced EdTech educator based in Chhattisgarh.
Our pedagogy is 100% practical, project-based learning. Students build games, templates, and layouts instead of memorizing definitions.

General.q: Is SkillNest aligned with school boards?
General.a: Yes, our digital literacy and coding programs are fully aligned with India's National Education Policy (NEP 2020) guidelines for schools.
SkillNest is headquartered in Durg / Bhilai Nagar, Chhattisgarh, India, but operates live cohorts online nationwide.

General.q: How many students have you trained?
General.a: We have trained over 1,000+ students across various states in India since our founding.

General.q: What is your customer satisfaction rate?
General.a: We maintain a 4.9/5 stars aggregate rating on Google based on 247+ verified parent reviews.

General.q: How is SkillNest different from traditional computer centers?
General.a: We teach modern, practical software skills and computational logic rather than textbook theory questions.

General.q: What is the typical batch size?
General.a: We limit our micro-batches to 5 to 10 students to ensure individual instructor attention.

General.q: Can students interact with each other in class?
General.a: Yes, our online classes encourage peer discussions, team projects, and cooperative coding reviews.

Enrollment.q: How can I enroll my child?
Enrollment.a: You can enroll by booking a free demo session on our website and discussing your child's schedule with our advisor.
Yes, we offer a free 1-on-1 trial and skill assessment session for every new student before payment.
Most program cohorts run for a duration of 6 weeks with regular weekly sessions.
Yes, we offer flexible slots including evening weekday batches and morning weekend cohorts.

Enrollment.q: What happens if my child misses a class?
Enrollment.a: Recorded lectures and supplementary guides are provided so students can catch up before the next session.

Enrollment.q: Can we change the batch schedule midway?
Enrollment.a: Yes, schedule changes can be coordinated with our student support team based on batch availability.
We offer a 100% money-back refund guarantee if you cancel after the first cohort session.
Classes are open to all school students between Class 3 and Class 10 (ages 8 to 16).
Yes, sibling enrollment discounts are offered and can be discussed during the demo booking.
Yes, we welcome students from all time zones who wish to follow our English-language cohort curricula.

Curriculum.q: What programming languages do you teach?
Curriculum.a: We focus on MIT Scratch block-coding for younger students to build core logic before transition to script coding.
Yes, our curriculum includes hands-on programs for Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

Curriculum.q: What is covered in typing lessons?
Curriculum.a: We teach home-row finger placement, correct postures, and speed drills to achieve 40+ WPM.
Yes, internet safety, cyber hygiene, and data security are integral to all our computer and AI courses.
Yes, students learn prompt frameworks (Role, Task, Context, Output) to use AI search and design helpers safely.
Yes, our Excel modules cover arithmetic calculations, formula logic, and data charts creation.

Curriculum.q: What projects do coding students build?
Curriculum.a: Students build interactive coordinate-based arcade games, math mazes, and custom animations.

Curriculum.q: What is the focus of presentation classes?
Curriculum.a: We teach students slide master layouts, Canva graphic integrations, and public speaking speech outlines.
Yes, file structures, desktop shortcuts, cloud folders, and clean file directories are foundational topics.
Yes, students who graduate from basic paths can join intermediate and advanced digital literacy cohorts.

Technical.q: What devices can my child use?
Technical.a: Students must use a laptop or desktop computer. Tablets, phones, or Chromebooks are not supported for typing/coding.

Technical.q: Do I need to buy expensive software?
Technical.a: No, we use free web-based platforms (Scratch, Canva) and standard tools. Microsoft Office or Google Docs is required.
A standard broadband or mobile hotspot connection (at least 5 Mbps) is sufficient for live video sharing.
Yes, webcams must be active during class so teachers can monitor engagement and guide students.

Technical.q: How does the teacher see my child's screen?
Technical.a: Teachers request screen sharing via the online meeting client to debug coding errors live.
Any recent version of Windows or macOS is suitable for our classes.
Yes, a physical mouse is highly recommended for file dragging, layout design, and coding.

Technical.q: Can classes be taken on a smart TV?
Technical.a: No, active keyboard input is mandatory for every class, so a smart TV will not work.
Projects are uploaded and shared via online platforms or cloud folder drives set up for each batch.

Technical.q: Who do I contact for technical issues?
Technical.a: Our support team is reachable via WhatsApp or at admin@skillnest.co.in for immediate setup help.

Pricing.q: Why is pricing not displayed on the website?
Pricing.a: We customize learning pathways based on the child's age and initial digital assessment, so we share quotes directly.
Yes, we support paying program fees in interest-free monthly installments.
Yes, our trial demo session is 100% free and requires no registration payment details.

Pricing.q: Do you charge extra for study materials?
Pricing.a: No, all learning templates, printable guides, and worksheets are included in the course fee.
No, the certificate review and digital delivery are fully included in the program fee.

Pricing.q: How can I pay for the courses?
Pricing.a: We accept major online modes including UPI, credit/debit cards, net banking, and digital wallets.

Pricing.q: Do you offer refunds for missed classes?
Pricing.a: No, but we provide makeup resources and recordings so the student receives the full content value.

Pricing.q: Is there a discount for group bookings?
Pricing.a: Yes, parents coordinating group registrations for 3+ kids receive custom cohort discounts.

Pricing.q: Are B2B school workshop rates different?
Pricing.a: Yes, we provide custom quotes for institutional partnerships. Reach out to admin@skillnest.co.in.

Pricing.q: Are taxes included in the fee quoted?
Pricing.a: Yes, all fees shared with parents are all-inclusive of applicable registration taxes.

Certification.a: Yes, students receive a digital Certificate of Completion upon concluding their 6-week cohort.
Students must complete at least 3 hands-on project submissions during the program duration.
Certificates are verified and emailed directly to parents in high-resolution PDF format.

Certification.q: Can the certificate be shared on LinkedIn?
Certification.a: Yes, the digital certificate includes credentials that can be added to parental social profiles.
Certificates are signed by founder Aanandita Uplopwar and the lead batch instructor.

Certification.q: Is there an exam at the end?
Certification.a: No, evaluation is based on continuous project creation rather than a final theoretical exam.
Our certificates highlight practical skill accomplishments aligned with NEP 2020 computer mandates.

Certification.q: Can I request a printed copy?
Certification.a: By default, we deliver high-res digital copies. Printed copies can be requested for a nominal delivery fee.

Certification.q: What happens if a project is resubmitted?
Certification.a: Teachers review and allow students to resubmit projects to achieve standard passing scores.
Yes, every certificate is accompanied by a detailed student performance and skill scorecard.
